_meta.notes: Verified against DE Wikipedia (VW Golf 6/7/8 articles), 2026-04-28. Mk6: added 1.6 TDI BlueMotion 110 KM and 1.6 BiFuel LPG; flagged 2.0 TDI 110 KM as medium � DE Wikipedia shows no 2.0 TDI at 81 kW for Mk6 (81 kW = 1.6 TDI territory). Mk7: added 1.2 TSI 110 KM FL (2016-18), 1.4 TGI 110 CNG (2015-16), 1.5 TGI 110 CNG pre-FL (2014-17); flagged 1.4 TSI 125 KM as medium � not in DE Wikipedia table. Mk8: added 1.0 TSI 90 KM, 1.2 TSI 115 KM, 1.4 TSI 150 KM, 2.0 TSI 190 KM, GTI 265 KM FL (2024+); GTI 245 KM year_to set to 2024 (replaced by 265 KM at facelift).

DE Wikipedia Volkswagen_Passat B6 section. 1.4/1.8 TSI from 2007 facelift; 1.6 TDI replaced 1.9 TDI at 2008 facelift. EcoFuel = CNG. 2.0 TSI consolidates TFSI/TSI badge.
DE Wikipedia Volkswagen_Passat B7 section. BlueTDI/BlueMotion Technology consolidated into standard TDI entries (same power). B7 = facelift of B6 platform.
DE Wikipedia Volkswagen_Passat B8 section. MQB platform. 1.5 TSI replaced 1.4 TSI at 2019 facelift. GTE = PHEV system output. 2.0 TDI 122/190/200 added at facelift.
DE Wikipedia Volkswagen_Passat B9 section. MQB Evo. 1.5 eTSI = 48V mild hybrid. eHybrid = PHEV system output. B9 estate-only in Europe (no saloon).

DE Wikipedia VW_Tiguan_I. 1.4 TSI 150 uses EA111 (2007-2015) and EA211 1395cc (2015-2016). 4Motion available on 1.4 TSI 150/160 optionally. EA189 diesel affected by Dieselgate (2015). DSG = 6- or 7-speed.

DE Wikipedia VW_Tiguan_II. Allspace (2017-2024) shares engine specs. 1.4 TSI eHybrid: ICE 110 kW + EM 85 kW = 180 kW system. WLTP-measured 2.0 TDI 150 (2018+) same engine as 2016-2018. DSG = 6-speed (eHybrid) or 7-speed (all others).

DE Wikipedia VW_Tiguan_III. EU launch 11/2023. 1.5 eTSI = EA211 evo2 with 48V mild hybrid. PHEV system power: ICE 110/130 kW + EM 85 kW; 19.7 kWh battery; ~118-125 km WLTP electric range. All diesel EA288 evo. No manual gearbox in Gen III.

DE Wikipedia VW_T-Roc_I. 1.0 TSI dropped at 2020 facelift. 1.5 TSI ACT cylinder deactivation from 11/2020; same 110 kW from launch. 1.5 TSI 190 KM = ACTplus evo2 EA211 from 02/2024. T-Roc R (300 PS) from 08/2019. 1.6 TDI available from 06/2018; 2.0 TDI 190 4Motion ended ~2022. No manual on 2.0 units. DSG = 7-speed.
Modern Octavia I-IV (1996-present); pre-1980 Octavia (1959 original) treated as separate entry.

Verified against German Wikipedia (Škoda Octavia II article, de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Škoda_Octavia_II), 2026-04-28. Covers full gen 05/2004–01/2013 incl. facelift from 11/2008. Corrections: 1.4 75 KM year_to 2010→2006; LPG power corrected to 72 kW/98 KM (LPG-mode). Added: 1.4 80 KM (2006–13), 1.8 TSI 152 KM (2009–13 detuned variant), 2.0 TDI 136 KM PD (2004–10), 2.0 TDI 110 KM CR (2010–13), 1.6 CNG 102 KM (2007–13). DPF-only and PD/CR sub-variants of same power consolidated.

Verified against German Wikipedia (Škoda Octavia III article, de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Škoda_Octavia_III), 2026-04-28. Pre-facelift 12/2012–11/2016, facelift 11/2016–2020. Corrections: 1.4 TSI G-TEC fixed to 77 kW/105 KM (manual only, to 2015); 1.8 TSI year_to corrected to 2018. Added: 1.4 TSI 110 KM (2015–2016 transitional petrol), 1.8 TSI 4×4, 2.0 TSI 190 KM, 2.0 TSI 4×4 220 KM (all facelift-era), 2.0 TDI 184 KM standard (to 2017; RS continues to 2020). Wikipedia confirmed diesel RS exists. Entries 1.2 TSI 105/110 KM and 1.4 TSI 140/150 KM ACT not confirmed by Wikipedia table — retained but may need further source verification.

Verified against German Wikipedia (Škoda Octavia IV article). Pre-facelift 11/2019-02/2024, facelift 14 Feb 2024. 1.0 TSI/TSI e-TEC dropped at facelift (replaced by 1.5 TSI). RS petrol uprated 245→265 KM at facelift; RS diesel and RS PHEV discontinued at facelift. CNG G-TEC was a very short 2020-only production run (March-December).
Octavia 4x4 raised-suspension trim, sold from Mk2 onward.
Fabia IV (2021-present) is a separate model not yet in catalog under this entry.
DE Wikipedia Skoda_Fabia_IV. Rally2 since 10/2024, limited production. 1.5 TSI 177 KM since 10/2025.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Superb (Gen I, 3U, 2001-2008). Based on VW Passat B5 platform. 1.8T = EA113 turbo. 2.0 = naturally aspirated. 2.8 V6 = 2771cc VR6. 1.9 TDI = PD pump-injector (EA188). 1.9 TDI 156 PS from ~2003; DSG = 6-speed. Wikidata ID covers full Superb model line.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Superb (Gen II, 3T, 2008-2015, incl. facelift 2013). PQ35 platform. 1.4 TSI = EA111. 1.8 TSI = EA888. 3.6 FSI = VR6 with DSG + 4x4 Haldex. 1.6 TDI from 2010 (EU5). 2.0 TDI = EA189 (Dieselgate engine). DSG = 6-speed.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Superb (Gen III, 3V, 2015-2024). MQB platform. 1.5 TSI replaces 1.4 TSI from 2019 facelift. 2.0 TSI 190 PS replaces 1.8 TSI. 1.4 TSI iV PHEV: ICE 115 kW + EM 85 kW = 160 kW system, 13 kWh, ~55 km EV. 1.6 TDI discontinued 2018. DSG = 7-speed.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Superb (Gen IV, 3Y, 2024-). MQB evo platform. All engines 7-speed DSG. 1.5 TSI mHEV = 48V mild hybrid. 1.5 TSI iV PHEV: 25.7 kWh battery, ~100 km WLTP EV range, 6-speed DSG, system 200 kW/272 PS. No manual gearbox in Gen IV.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Kamiq (Mk1 06/2019-). MQB-A0 platform. FWD only, no AWD. 1.0 TSI 90 PS = base (66 kW, pre-2024 only); 95 PS = main variant (70 kW). 1.5 TSI 150 PS = ACT cylinder deactivation. G-TEC (CNG) very brief production 01/2020-10/2020. 1.6 TDI discontinued 11/2020. Facelift 08/2023. DSG = 7-speed.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Kodiaq (Mk1 2016-2024). MQB platform, up to 7 seats. 1.4 TSI replaced by 1.5 TSI from 2018. 2.0 TSI 180 PS replaced by ACT 190 PS at 2019 facelift. vRS: 245 PS petrol from 2021, 240 PS diesel from 2019. DSG = 7-speed. Greentech = same engine, low-emission variant; merged with standard 150 TDI.

DE Wikipedia Skoda_Kodiaq (Mk2 12/2023-). MQB evo platform. All DSG, no manual. 1.5 TSI mHEV = 48V mild hybrid. iV PHEV = 25.7 kWh battery, ~100 km WLTP EV, 6-speed DSG, FWD. RS = 265 PS replaces 245 PS vRS. Diesel 193 PS 4x4 replaces 190 PS unit.

European market engines compiled from training knowledge + cross-checked against EN/JA Wikipedia, 2026-04-28. DE Wikipedia has no structured table for this generation. 1.8 VVT-i and 2.2 D-4D removed � not confirmed for European Corolla E150 (those were Avensis/US-market engines). 2.0 D-4D (1CD-FTV) confirmed for full 2007�2013 run. All entries remain medium confidence pending a Toyota-specific European source.

Verified against DE Wikipedia (section 34) and cross-checked with EN Wikipedia, 2026-04-28. DE Wikipedia confirmed 3 engines; EN Wikipedia revealed 2 additional European variants: 1.6 Dual VVT-i (non-Valvematic, 91 kW) and 1.8 Dual VVT-i (103 kW, Multidrive S CVT). These two added at medium confidence � power figures inferred from engine codes, exact PL availability unconfirmed. 1.33 and 1.4 D-4D discontinued at 2016 mid-cycle refresh.

Verified against DE Wikipedia (section 39) and cross-checked with EN Wikipedia, 2026-04-28. Facelift confirmed as 2023 (HSD Gen 5 per EN Wikipedia). 1.2T petrol discontinued 2020. 1.8 HEV 122 KM (pre-facelift 2019�2023), 1.8 HEV 140 KM (facelift 2023�). 2.0 HEV 196 KM confirmed post-facelift 2023�; medium confidence � DE Wikipedia listed since 02/2025 (possibly German market launch date).
DE Wikipedia Toyota_Yaris_(XP13). Hybrid system output 74 kW. Diesel dropped after 04/2017.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_Yaris_(XP21). Hybrid system output. GR Yaris is separate performance model on XP210 platform.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_Yaris_Cross Technische Daten. System power used for hybrids. E-Four = rear e-motor AWD. Petrol dropped at 04/2024 facelift. EU availability from 09/2021.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_C-HR Mk1 section. Non-EU 2.0 petrol and China EV skipped. 1.2T dropped at 09/2020 EU refresh; 4x4 variant to 11/2019 only. System power for hybrids.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_C-HR Mk2 section. EU launch 01/2024. AWD via rear e-motor (30 kW); system power same badge as FWD. PHEV system output.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_RAV4 XA40 section. EU launch 04/2013. 2.5 NA petrol (Russia/USA only) skipped. 2.5 Hybrid from 2016 facelift only. System power for hybrid.
DE Wikipedia Toyota_RAV4 XA50 section. EU launch 01/2019. 2.0 petrol dropped from EU after 05/2020. 2.5 NA and 2.5 AWD petrol (Russia/NA only) skipped. System power for HEV/PHEV.

DE Wikipedia Toyota_Auris (E150 facelift, 2010-2012). MC = 2010. 1.33 = 1NR-FE. 1.6/1.8 Valvematic = 1ZR-FAE/2ZR-FAE. HSD = 2ZR-FXE + electric motor, system 100 kW. 1.4 D-4D = 1ND-TV. 2.0 D-4D = 1AD-FTV. 2.2 D-CAT = 2AD-FHV. automatic on 1.6 = Multidrive S (single-clutch AMT).

DE Wikipedia Toyota_Auris (E180, 2012-2018). Facelift 2015. 1.2 Turbo = 8NR-FTS (added at FL). 1.33 = 1NR-FE. 1.6 Valvematic = 1ZR-FAE (pre-FL only). HSD = 2ZR-FXE hybrid, system 100 kW. 1.4 D-4D = 1ND-TV; 1.6 D-4D = 1WW-WTV (added at FL). 2.0 D-4D = 1AD-FTV (pre-FL). automatic on 1.6 petrol = Multidrive S.
DE Wikipedia Opel_Astra_H. LPG variants common in Poland.
DE Wikipedia Opel_Astra_J.
DE Wikipedia Opel_Astra_K.
DE Wikipedia Opel_Astra_L. Electric = Astra-e variant.

DE Wikipedia Opel_Corsa_D (07/2006-10/2014). 1.0 dropped 2009; 1.2 replaced it. 1.4 power bump 2010 (90→101 PS). LPG power listed in petrol mode. 1.3 CDTI = Fiat-derived MultiJet. automatic = 4-speed. Easytronic (AMT) on 1.2/1.3 CDTI skipped (very rare). Wikidata ID covers full Corsa model line.

DE Wikipedia Opel_Corsa_E (11/2014-06/2019). 1.0 SIDI Turbo = 3-cyl. Two 1.4 variants: 1398cc NA 70PS and 1364cc ecoFlex 90PS (different block). OPC up from 192 PS. 1.3 CDTI 75 PS to 2018, 95 PS to 2017. automatic = 6-speed.

DE Wikipedia Opel_Corsa_F (07/2019-). PSA/Stellantis platform (CMP). All petrol = 3-cyl PureTech. mHEV = 48V 6-speed DSG from 02/2024 facelift. 1.5 CDTI discontinued 06/2023. EV = Vitesco EMR3; 50 kWh (2019-2023) then 51 kWh (2023+). automatic (EV) = single-speed reduction gear.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A4_B8. Multitronic mapped to 'automatic'. E85 flex variant skipped. S-tronic mapped to 'DSG'.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A4_B9. S-tronic=DSG, tiptronic=automatic. 45 TFSI power bump 245→265 KM at MY2020 split into two entries. g-tron=CNG primary fuel.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A5_8T. Multitronic/S-tronic=DSG/automatic. 2.0 TDI 143 KM = 105 kW CAGA engine code.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A5_F5. S-tronic=DSG, tiptronic=automatic. 40 TFSI power bumped 190→204 KM at MY2020. S5 TDI 341 KM (251 kW) post-2020 variant consolidated into 347 KM entry.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A5_B10. PHEV power = system output. RS5 e-hybrid since 04/2026.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A3_8P facelift section. 1.2 TFSI from 2010; RS3 from 2011; 1.9 TDI discontinued ~2010. DSG = S-tronic.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A3_8V section. DSG = S-tronic. g-tron = CNG. e-tron = PHEV system output. 1.0/1.5 TFSI from 2016 facelift.
DE Wikipedia Audi_A3_8Y section. All-DSG (S-tronic), no manual offered. Badge numbers: 30=110PS/1.0T, 35=150PS/1.5T, 40=190PS+/2.0T. PHEV system output. g-tron = CNG.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C6 (facelift 2007-2011). 2.8 FSI up from 190 PS pre-facelift. 3.0 TFSI supercharged. CVT = multitronic; automatic = tiptronic. Wikidata ID covers full A6 model line; generation-specific ID not extracted.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C7 (2011-2018). 1.8 TFSI added 09/2014 facelift; 3.0 TFSI supercharged dropped 2012. Hybrid = 2.0 TFSI + 40 kW EM, system 180 kW. DSG = 7-speed S-tronic. automatic = 8-speed tiptronic. 3.0 TDI 326 = biturbo. S6/RS6 excluded.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C8 (2018-). All engines 48V mHEV. New numerical badge system: 35/40/45/50/55. DSG = 7-speed S-tronic; automatic = 8-speed tiptronic (PHEV). 55 TFSI e PHEV: ICE 195 kW + EM 105 kW ≈ 270 kW / 367 PS system. Diesel range ended ~2023. S6 TDI/RS6 excluded.

DE Wikipedia Audi_A6_C6 (pre-facelift 02/2004-08/2008). 2.0 TFSI from 04/2005. 2.8 FSI 209 PS from 10/2006 (replaced 190 PS 2.4 V6). 4.2 FSI quattro continues into facelift (year_to 2010). 3.0 TDI power bump 04/2006 (224→233 PS). CVT = multitronic; automatic = tiptronic. S6 (V10)/RS6 excluded.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 21/22. Covers full Mk2/'05 gen incl. 2008 facelift. Flexifuel/RS500 skipped.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 33/34. Ford PowerShift DCT mapped to DSG.
DE Wikipedia Ford_Focus section 39/40. 1.0 EcoBoost Hybrid = mild hybrid (48V). Focus discontinued 11/2025.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_'09 section. Pre-facelift 2008-2012, facelift 2012-2017. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. ST rated at 182 PS (147 kW overboost to 200 PS); ST200 rated at 200 PS (215 overboost). 1.0 NA 65/80 PS = naturally aspirated Ti-VCT.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Fiesta_'18 section. EU launch 2017. 1.1 NA 3-cylinder replaces 1.25. 1.0 EcoBoost 85 PS dropped after 2019 facelift. 1.5 EcoBoost ST replaces 1.6 unit. automatic = 7-speed DCT on 100 PS variant.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk1 02/2008-10/2012). EU-only model (US = Escape). 2.5 T5 petrol = Volvo-derived. TDCi 136 PS replaced by 140/163 PS from 02/2010 facelift. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. 4WD optional. Produced in Saarlouis. Wikidata ID covers full Kuga model line.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk2 11/2012-06/2019). 1.6 EcoBoost replaced by 1.5 EcoBoost from 2016 facelift (150 PS dropped). 2.0 EcoBoost 242 PS added 11/2017. DSG = 6-speed PowerShift. 4x4 standard on top petrol/diesel variants.

DE Wikipedia Ford_Kuga (Mk3 2019-, production from 04/2020). FHEV = 2.5 Atkinson + 95 kW EM, system ~150 kW/190 PS, discontinued 2024. PHEV = 14.4 kWh, 56 km WLTP EV range, system 165 kW/225 PS. All diesel discontinued 02/2022. automatic = 8-speed. CVT = Ford e-CVT (Atkinson hybrid).
DE Wikipedia BMW_E90. Covers E90/E91/E92/E93 family. 323i had two power outputs (N52B25). M3 = V8 4.0L.
DE Wikipedia BMW_F30 sections 33/38. M DCT mapped to DSG. 330e = PHEV system output.
DE Wikipedia BMW_G20. 318d/320d got 48V mild hybrid from 02/2020. PHEV system output. M DCT mapped to DSG.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_F10 (01/2010-01/2017). 520i/528i use N20 4-cyl (from 2011); earlier 528i was N53 6-cyl 258PS. 520d: N47 engine to 2014 (184PS), then B47 (190PS). 525d = N47 biturbo 4-cyl. 535i = N55 6-cyl. ActiveHybrid 5 = N55 + 40kW EM. automatic = 8-speed ZF. Wikidata ID covers full 5 Series model line; F10-specific ID not extracted.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_G30 (02/2017-08/2023). All B-series modular engines. 530i = B48 4-cyl; 540i/545e = B58 6-cyl. 530e PHEV: ICE 135 kW + EM 83 kW = 185 kW system. 545e: ICE 210 kW + EM 80 kW = 290 kW system. 520d manual to summer 2018 only. 525d discontinued 2019. LCI facelift 07/2020.

DE Wikipedia BMW_5er_G60 (launch 10/2023). All ICE = 48V mild hybrid. 530e PHEV: ICE 140 kW + EM 135 kW = 220 kW system. 550e: ICE 230 kW + EM 145 kW = 360 kW system. i5 shares G60 platform. 530i/540i petrol not available in EU (US/Asia only). automatic = 8-speed ZF. M5 PHEV (727PS) excluded.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Tucson sections 6/7. 1.6 CRDi from 2018 facelift; 1.7 CRDi pre-facelift. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_Tucson sections 10/11/12. 48V mild-hybrid petrol variants share power badges. HEV/PHEV = system output. iMT mapped to manual.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(FD). 2.0 CVVT and 2.0 CRDi dropped 2010 (pre-Euro 5). 1.6 CRDi 128 PS not available in DE — skipped. automatic = 4-speed.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(GD). automatic = 6-step; DSG = 7-speed DCT (from 04/2015 for GDI/CRDi 110). 1.4 blue = updated 1368 cc Gamma. 1.6 GDI Turbo = i30 Turbo Sport trim.

DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(PD). DSG = 7-speed DCT (8-speed DCT for N Performance 280 optional). 1.5 DPI = naturally aspirated 1.5. Diesel range ended 01/2022. N variants production gap 04/2019–05/2021.
DE Wikipedia Hyundai_i30_(PD) section 06/2024+ (CN7 not yet a separate DE Wikipedia article). No diesel. 1.0 T-GDI replaces 1.0 120 PS; 1.5 T-GDI 140 PS replaces 160 PS as main powertrain.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage SL section. 2.0 CVVT 150 KM (Mexico/Russia) skipped. AWD standard on 2.0 CRDi 184.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage QL section. 1.6 CRDi from 2018 facelift replacing 1.7 CRDi. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Kia_Sportage NQ5 section. 48V mild-hybrid shares power badges with regular T-GDI. PHEV system power; 3 variants across model years. HEV = 6-AT.

DE Wikipedia Kia_cee'd (JD, 04/2012-08/2018). 1.0 T-GDI and 1.6 CRDi 136 added at 06/2015 facelift. DSG = 6-speed DCT (petrol GT, CRDi 136). automatic = 6-speed torque converter (CRDi 110). Wikidata ID covers full cee'd/Ceed model line; generation-specific ID not found in DE article.

DE Wikipedia Kia_Ceed (CD, 06/2018-). 1.4 CVVT brief launch only (→ 1.4 T-GDI). 1.5 T-GDI replaces 1.4 T-GDI from 2021 facelift. HEV = 77 kW ICE + 44 kW EM, system 104 kW, hatchback only. PHEV = same system + 9.3–11.2 kWh battery, SW wagon only. New CRDi 1598 cc (U2 diesel) vs JD's 1582 cc. DSG = 6- or 7-speed DCT.

DE Wikipedia Kia_K4. EU launch 11/2025 (hatchback); SW Sportswagon 01/2026. Replaces Kia Ceed (CD). 1.0 T-GDI also available as 48V mHEV at same 85 kW — merged into one entry. 1.6 T-GDI offered in 150 PS and 180 PS tunes. No diesel or PHEV in EU lineup. NA variants excluded. DSG = 7-speed DCT.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio III facelift section. Only facelift-era verified engines included; base 1.2 16V/1.4 16V carry-overs not in DE section.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio IV section. DSG = 6-speed EDC dual-clutch. 0.9 TCe = 3-cylinder turbo.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Clio V section. E-Tech = full HEV, system power, DHT mapped to automatic. DSG = 7-speed EDC. 1.3 TCe 67/72 KM are detuned fleet variants.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane III section. 2.0 TCe 190 KM (brief 2012-13) merged into 180/220 range. DSG = 6-speed EDC. 2.0 16V uses CVT.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane IV section. DSG = 7-speed EDC. E-Tech PHEV system output. 1.6 dCi 165 badge = 163 PS actual.
DE Wikipedia Renault_Megane E-Tech section. EV40 = 40 kWh/300 km WLTP; EV60 = 60 kWh/450-470 km WLTP. All FWD. displacement_cc=0 (no ICE).

EN/FR Wikipedia Renault_Captur (Gen I J87, 2013-2019). B0 platform. TCe 90 = H4Bt 3-cyl. TCe 120 = H4Jt 4-cyl. TCe 130 = H5Ht 1.3T from 2017 facelift. dCi = K9K 1.5 diesel. EDC = 6-speed dual-clutch, labeled DSG.

EN/FR Wikipedia Renault_Captur (Gen II XJB, 2019-). CMF-B platform. TCe 90 = H4Dt 1.0T 3-cyl. TCe 140 = H5Ht 1.3T 4-cyl. E-Tech Hybrid: 1.6 Atkinson + 2 electric motors, system 105kW/145PS, no plug. E-Tech PHEV: system 118kW/160PS, 9.8kWh, ~45km EV. Blue dCi = K9K EU6d, phased out ~2023. EDC = DSG.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster I section. 4x2 and 4x4 share engine specs. dCi 86 very brief (03-09/2010). dCi 110 badge = 107-110 PS across production; 80 kW from 10/2013.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster II section. DSG = 6-speed EDC. ECO-G = bifuel LPG/petrol. 1.2 TCe 125 and 1.6 SCe very brief (01-09/2018 transition). 1.3 TCe 130 badge = 131 PS actual.
DE Wikipedia Dacia_Duster III section. No diesel. ECO-G = LPG/petrol bifuel. Mild Hybrid = 48V starter-generator. Full Hybrid = system power. Hybrid-G = LPG + hybrid AWD.

DE Wikipedia Dacia_Sandero (Gen II B52, 2012-2020). Facelift 2016. 0.9 TCe = H4Bt 3-cyl turbo. 1.2 16V = D4F pre-facelift only. 1.0 SCe = H4D naturally aspirated (facelift). 0.9 TCe Bifuel = LPG+petrol dual-fuel system. 1.5 dCi = K9K; 95 PS from 2018 (EU6c). No automatic on diesel. Easy-R automated-manual offered but negligible volume.

DE Wikipedia Dacia_Sandero (Gen III BJI, 2020-). CMF-B platform. No diesel. 1.0 SCe = naturally aspirated. 1.0 TCe = turbocharged (H4D-H series). ECO-G = bifuel LPG+petrol. CVT = X-Tronic on 1.0 TCe 90. 1.0 TCe 100 PS petrol (non-ECO-G) from 2022 facelift. Facelift 2022 added mild cosmetic updates.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_204. C 180 uses M271/M274 (1597-1595 cc); 1595 cc used as final spec. C 200/C 250 use M271 1796 cc. automatic = 5G/7G-Tronic. C 300 covers C 280 (2007-09) and C 300 (2009-11). C 350 CDI 231 covers C 320 CDI (2007-09) and C 300/350 CDI (2009-14). 4MATIC available on C 220/250/350 CDI.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_205. C 180/200 post-07/2018 use M264 1497 cc with EQ Boost (mHEV); badge power unchanged. C 200 d and C 220 d engines swapped to OM654 (1597/1950 cc) from 07/2018 facelift. PHEV = system power. automatic = 7G/9G-Tronic. 4MATIC available on C 200/300/220d/250d/300d.

DE Wikipedia Mercedes-Benz_Baureihe_206. All petrol and diesel are 48V EQ Boost mHEV (ICE + 17 kW e-motor); power listed = ICE only (badge). All 9G-Tronic. C 200 4MATIC shares spec with C 200 RWD. AMG C 63 S E Performance (680 PS PHEV, 2023-) omitted as extreme outlier.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J10 facelift 03/2010-11/2013). 1.6 petrol up from 114 PS. 1.6 dCi added 2011. 2.0 engines available with 4WD option. CVT = X-TRONIC; DSG = auto on diesel. Wikidata covers full J10 generation.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J11 2013-2021). 1.2 DIG-T dropped 08/2018; replaced by 1.3 DIG-T (co-developed with Renault). 1.6 dCi replaced by 1.7 dCi (R9N) from 2019. 1.3 DIG-T 160 PS = late J11 pre-J12 bridge variant. DSG = 7-speed DCT (petrol) or auto (diesel). 4WD available on 1.3 140/1.6 dCi/1.7 dCi.

DE Wikipedia Nissan_Qashqai (J12 06/2021-). No diesel. All petrol = 12V mHEV. e-Power = serial hybrid: 1.5 VC-T (variable compression) ICE acts as generator only; 140 kW electric motor drives wheels; listed power = motor output. CVT = X-TRONIC e-CVT (e-Power) or X-TRONIC (mHEV). 4WD option on 1.3 160 and e-Power.

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC60 (Mk1 Y20, 2008-2017). T5/T6 Drive-E (1969cc 4-cyl) from 2014 facelift; original T6 = B6324S 2953cc inline-6 supercharged. 3.2 = B6324S2 NA 6-cyl. D3/D4 = D5204 4-cyl; D5 = D5244 5-cyl biturbo (2400cc). automatic = Geartronic 6-speed (pre-fl) or 8-speed (post-fl).

DE Wikipedia Volvo_XC60 (Mk2 SPA, 2017-). All Drive-E 2.0T (1969cc). T→B rebadging 2020 with 48V mHEV integration (same power). T8 PHEV: ICE 228 kW + EM 107 kW rear axle, system 287 kW/390 PS; 14.9 kWh battery; ~50 km WLTP EV; Recharge branding from 09/2021. Diesel phased out ~2023. automatic = 8-speed Geartronic.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (9N 2001-2005, 9N3 facelift 2005-2009). 1.2 = AZQ/BME 3-cyl. 1.4 16V = BBY/BKY. GTI = 9N3 only, 1.8T AWP/BUD. 1.9 SDI = 9N only. 1.4 TDI = AMF/BNM PD; 9N3 switched to CR. 1.9 TDI = ATD/BLT PD. automatic = 4-speed Tiptronic on petrol.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (6R pre-facelift, 2009-2014). PQ25 platform. 1.2 MPI = CGP 3-cyl. 1.2 TSI = CBZ twin-charged (supercharged+turbo). 1.4 TSI GTI = CAVE twin-charged 180 PS, DSG only (discontinued 2013). 1.2 TDI = CFWA 3-cyl BlueMotion. 1.4/1.6 TDI = CR. DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (6C facelift, 2014-2017). 1.0 MPI/TSI = EA111 3-cyl replacing 1.2 units. 1.4 TSI BlueGT = EA211 with cylinder deactivation. 1.8 TSI GTI = EA888 replacing twin-charged 1.4 TSI. 1.4 TDI = EA288 3-cyl. DSG = 7-speed dry.

DE Wikipedia VW_Polo (AW, 2017-). MQB-A0 platform. 1.0 TSI = EA211 evo. 1.5 TSI ACT = EA211 evo with cylinder deactivation (from 2020 facelift). GTI: 147kW/200PS (2017-2021), 152kW/207PS (2022+ FL). 1.0 TGI = CNG+petrol bifuel. 1.6 TDI discontinued ~2020 (EU6d). DSG = 7-speed dry.
